data.title: Luxury goods market projected to reach $704.95 billion by 2033 from $382.8 billion in 2024, with a 7.02% CAGR growth.
data.description: A report titled "Luxury Goods Market Overview and Forecast 2025-2033" has recently been included in ResearchAndMarkets.com's offerings. The Luxury Goods Market is projected to grow significantly, reaching US$704.95 billion by 2033, up from US$382.8 billion in 2024. The industry is expected to experience a Compound Annual Growth Rate (CAGR) of 7.02% from 2025 to 2033. Several factors are driving this growth, including rising disposable incomes, a growing middle-class consumer base, increased brand recognition, digital transformations, expansion into emerging markets, a preference for experiential luxury, influencer marketing, and a focus on sustainability.

data.title: Nasdaq up 150 points to 21,345.90; ConocoPhillips beats earnings estimates. Tech sector rises 1%, healthcare falls 1.7%.
data.description: U.S. stocks saw mixed performance, with the Nasdaq Composite gaining over 150 points on Thursday morning. The Dow slightly decreased by 0.04% to 44,174.56, while the NASDAQ climbed 0.83% to 21,345.90, and the S&P 500 rose 0.32% to 6,365.19. Information technology shares surged by 1%, whereas health care stocks dropped by 1.7%. ConocoPhillips reported Q2 earnings of $1.42 per share, surpassing the estimated $1.40, with quarterly sales of $14.740 billion, outperforming the estimated $14.626 billion. Notably, CIMG Inc. shares surged by 180% to $0.56. This and more available on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Dogecoin surges 7% in a day, price at $0.2143 with $32.2 billion market cap. Trader warns of potential trap at $0.2079.
data.description: Dogecoin (DOGE) experienced a significant one-day surge, sparking mixed sentiments among traders. The meme coin's recent uptrend has attracted attention for both investment opportunities and potential risks. As of the latest data, DOGE is priced at $0.2143 with a market capitalization of $32.2 billion, showing a 7-day trend of -1.9%. Other cryptocurrencies like Shiba Inu (SHIB) and Pepe have also seen fluctuations in their prices, standing at $0.00001266 with a market cap of $7.5 billion and $0.00001093 with a market cap of $4.6 billion, respectively, each with their own 7-day trends. Notably, analyst Ali Martinez pointed out that DOGE is currently in a historically strong buying zone, with past patterns indicating potential for significant bull runs. Traders are advised to weigh the optimistic outlook with caution. For further details, the complete article can be found on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Backblaze reports strong Q2 2025 results: 29% B2 cloud storage revenue growth, 16% total revenue growth.
data.description: In an announcement from SAN MATEO, California, Backblaze, Inc. (Nasdaq: BLZE) revealed its financial performance for the second quarter of 2025. The company reported a 29% increase in revenue specifically in B2 Cloud Storage and a 16% revenue growth overall during Q2. Notably, there was a 30% rise year over year in customers contributing over $50,000 in ARR, an increase from the previously reported 53%. This data reflects the company's up-market momentum.

data.title: Papa John's stock rose premarket after reporting Q2 earnings of 41 cents per share, $529.166M in sales (+4.2%).
data.description: Papa John’s International, Inc. saw a rise in its shares trading higher before market hours on Thursday. The company posted adjusted earnings per share for the second quarter at 41 cents, surpassing the expected 34 cents. Quarterly sales stood at $529.166 million, marking a 4.2% year-over-year increase, surpassing analyst projections of $515.601 million. President and CEO Todd Penegor commented, "We returned to comparable sales growth in North America and achieved strong sales growth internationally, driven by transaction gains as we win more customer visits with a focus on our core pizza business." The revenue boost was mainly due to a $20.3 million surge in Commissary revenues, reflecting higher volumes and pricing during the quarter.
